preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 49% to win, well ahead of Czech Republic's 20.9% chance. This represents a clear lean toward the higher-rated team in the matchup. Czech Republic's path to victory remains modest under the statistical forecast.

The draw probability of 30% sits at a meaningful level, suggesting competitive balance in the expected scorelines. The gap between South Korea's win chance and a Czech Republic victory is substantial—roughly 28 percentage points—indicating the model sees this as a moderately decisive matchup rather than a close contest, though a stalemate remains plausible.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge notably. Bookmakers assess South Korea's win probability at 35.4%, some 14 percentage points lower than the model's 49%. They rate Czech Republic at 34.6%, nearly double the model's 20.9%. Both sources agree a draw is likely around 30%, but they sharply disagree on the two-team split. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is correct.
